executiveSummary: PrecisionTech Manufacturing's Salesforce implementation carries the legacy of a 2022 ERP migration that was executed under time pressure and left significant data and automation debt. The assessment found three hard blockers: a Contact dataset where 11% of records are duplicates and core fields are largely empty from poor ERP field mapping; 14 active Process Builder automations that were never migrated to Flow; and a knowledge base of zero published articles. None of these blockers are technically complex — they are remediation tasks that require focused effort over a defined sprint. The overall index of 49/100 places PrecisionTech in the 'Conditionally Ready' band. With the three blockers resolved, the projected index is 84 — a score that would make PrecisionTech a strong Agentforce candidate for AI-assisted opportunity management and quote-to-close acceleration.

domainSummaries.Data quality & completeness: Contact data is the most significant finding in this domain. The 2022 ERP migration imported 18,000 Contact records but did not map Email (38% complete), Phone (28%), or Title (20%) fields from the source system. Additionally, 11.2% of Contact records are duplicates — a direct consequence of the migration running in two batches without a deduplication step. Account data shows similar gaps: Industry (45%) and Website (32%) were not part of the original migration scope. Before Agentforce can accurately personalise outreach or match contacts to opportunities, the Contact dataset needs enrichment and deduplication.
domainSummaries.Automation health & conflicts: PrecisionTech has 34 active flows but also has 14 active Process Builder automations that were never migrated — the highest count seen across assessments this year. Process Builder was retired by Salesforce in 2023; these automations are running on unsupported infrastructure and cannot be extended or debugged reliably. 10 of the 34 flows (29%) are missing fault connectors, which means any unhandled error causes the automation to silently fail. Apex test coverage is 55%, below the floor required for a production AI environment. The automation estate needs a structured migration sprint before Agentforce is layered on top.
domainSummaries.Security & permission model: The Security Health Check score is 72/100. Three critical risks were flagged, all related to session security settings (timeout thresholds and login IP restrictions). No guest user risk was detected. The Salesforce administrator can resolve all three flags within a half-day configuration session. This domain is not a hard blocker and should be scheduled as a quick-win item in Week 1 of the remediation roadmap.
domainSummaries.Knowledge base & grounding: PrecisionTech has no published Knowledge articles. This is a hard blocker for any AI agent deployment — Agentforce requires a structured knowledge base to ground its responses and avoid hallucination. The scan identified 20 distinct case reason categories, with Order Delay (21%), Wrong Item Shipped (16%), and Defective Product (14%) accounting for over half of all case volume. The knowledge base build is the largest single effort item on the roadmap (estimated 8 person-days) but also the highest-value: a well-structured KB covering the top 10 reasons is the foundation for everything else in the Agentforce deployment.
domainSummaries.Metadata & technical debt: PrecisionTech has 420 unmanaged custom fields — above the 400-field advisory threshold. These accumulated from two sources: a 2019 CPQ implementation that was subsequently decommissioned, and the 2022 ERP migration which created duplicate object schemas. The CPQ-related fields (estimated 280) are entirely unused and should be retired through the governance process. No abandoned AppExchange packages were found. The field sprawl creates noise in the Salesforce UI and in any AI training context; a targeted cleanup is recommended as a medium-priority item.
domainSummaries.User adoption & process alignment: 67% of PrecisionTech's 180 licensed users logged in within the last 90 days. The operations director confirmed that approximately 30 users (field sales, based at customer sites) use the Salesforce mobile app rather than the desktop, which may undercount them in the LoginHistory metric. Average task activity is 4 per user per 90 days — below the industry median of 8. The sales operations team is actively working on adoption: a new quoting workflow launched in April 2026 has improved usage among inside sales. Adoption is not a hard blocker but should be monitored during the Agentforce pilot phase.
domainSummaries.Platform limits & API headroom: API usage is at 12% of the daily limit. Data storage is at 22% — elevated compared to the industry median, likely a residue of the ERP migration importing historical records. File storage is at 15%. There is sufficient headroom for the Agentforce deployment, though the storage figure should be reviewed as part of the data cleanup initiative. No action required before the pilot.
